Short stay visas (Schengen)
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3caP3HRojJjVMrRq9tVjUV/da44dbb7233711a1380d234513aac6e2/tourism-checklist-2024.pdf) for the Tourist Visa checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:Tourist Visa]
Please  note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 38.30 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash or card (all applicable taxes included).
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The application must be submitted at least 15 days before the intended travel date. Failing to do so will result in the application to be returned. The processing time can be up to 15 calendar days from the next working day of application submission 
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Consulate General of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant. Third Countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation follow the below link: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/uae/english/dubai/pdf/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/uae/english/dubai/pdf/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf)
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission. 
When booking an appointment for an Italy visa in Dubai, please note the following:
- Each user ID, including the associated email and mobile number, can be used to book only one appointment.
- Families with minors under the age of 12 who do not have an individual email address may bring the minor along with the parents’ confirmed appointment.
- A birth certificate or any document proving the relationship between the minor and the parents will be verified at the Visa Application Centre before entry is permitted.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission. 
When booking an appointment for an Italy visa in Dubai, please note the following:
- Each user ID, including the associated email and mobile number, can be used to book only one appointment.
- Families with minors under the age of 12 who do not have an individual email address may bring the minor along with the parents’ confirmed appointment.
- A birth certificate or any document proving the relationship between the minor and the parents will be verified at the Visa Application Centre before entry is permitted.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Tourist Visa]
Please  note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 38.30 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash or card (all applicable taxes included).
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Short stay visas (Schengen)
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3caP3HRojJjVMrRq9tVjUV/da44dbb7233711a1380d234513aac6e2/tourism-checklist-2024.pdf) for the Tourist Visa checklist

## Photo Specifications

Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:  General  The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The application must be submitted at least 15 days before the intended travel date. Failing to do so will result in the application to be returned. The processing time can be up to 15 calendar days from the next working day of application submission 
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Consulate General of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant. Third Countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation follow the below link: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/uae/english/dubai/pdf/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/uae/english/dubai/pdf/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf)
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

## Download Forms

[Table:Tourist Visa]

Digital Nomad visa applicants can now apply for their visa at VFS Italy visa application center in Dubai. Applicants must check for the availability of all the documents listed on the Digital Nomad Visa Document Checklist before booking an appointment.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Digital Nomad visa applicants can now apply for their visa at VFS Italy visa application center in Dubai. Applicants must check for the availability of all the documents listed on the Digital Nomad Visa Document Checklist before booking an appointment.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Digital Nomad Visa]
Please  note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 38.30 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash or card (all applicable taxes included).
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Long stay visas (National)
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7KCdJY16EljgHm7sr8eaBj/70425160051d24e88922ffd80c0db936/lavoroautonomo-nomadidigitali-checklist.pdf) for the Digital Nomad Visa checklist 

## Photo Specifications

Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:  General  The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The application must be submitted at least 15 days before the intended travel date. Failing to do so will result in the application to be returned. The processing time can be up to 90 calendar days from the next working day of application submission
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Consulate General of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant. Third Countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation follow the below link: https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/uae/english/dubai/pdf/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/uae/english/dubai/pdf/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf)
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Student Visa applicants for the academic year 2022/2023 can now apply for their visa at VFS Italy visa application center in Dubai. Students must check for the availability of all the documents listed on the Student Visa Document Checklist before booking an appointment.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission. 
Important Information:
Please note that the applications for study entry visa must be submitted no later than 29th November 2024 for:
- Laurea o Laurea magistrale (two-year or single-cycle);
- single academic courses
- courses of the Institutions of High Artistic, Musical and Coreutic Education (AFAM)
- courses of the Higher Schools of Linguistic Mediation (SSML),
- international mobility programmes (e.g. Erasmus, Marco Polo and Turandot)
- courses provided by the Institutes of Specialisation in Psychotherapy
On the contrary, Master degrees, Phd, Foundation courses may be submitted all year around, according to the scheduled beginning of the course itself.
Any change in the above terms will be communicated.
When booking an appointment for an Italy visa in Dubai, please note the following:
- Each user ID, including the associated email and mobile number, can be used to book only one appointment.
- Families with minors under the age of 12 who do not have an individual email address may bring the minor along with the parents’ confirmed appointment.
- A birth certificate or any document proving the relationship between the minor and the parents will be verified at the Visa Application Centre before entry is permitted.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Student Visa applicants for the academic year 2022/2023 can now apply for their visa at VFS Italy visa application center in Dubai. Students must check for the availability of all the documents listed on the Student Visa Document Checklist before booking an appointment.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission. 
Important Information:
Please note that the applications for study entry visa must be submitted no later than 29th November 2024 for:
- Laurea o Laurea magistrale (two-year or single-cycle);
- single academic courses
- courses of the Institutions of High Artistic, Musical and Coreutic Education (AFAM)
- courses of the Higher Schools of Linguistic Mediation (SSML),
- international mobility programmes (e.g. Erasmus, Marco Polo and Turandot)
- courses provided by the Institutes of Specialisation in Psychotherapy
On the contrary, Master degrees, Phd, Foundation courses may be submitted all year around, according to the scheduled beginning of the course itself.
Any change in the above terms will be communicated.
When booking an appointment for an Italy visa in Dubai, please note the following:
- Each user ID, including the associated email and mobile number, can be used to book only one appointment.
- Families with minors under the age of 12 who do not have an individual email address may bring the minor along with the parents’ confirmed appointment.
- A birth certificate or any document proving the relationship between the minor and the parents will be verified at the Visa Application Centre before entry is permitted.

## Visa Fees

[Table:Student]
Please  note:
- Please be informed that the fees charged by the Consulate General or VFS Global are based on the current exchange rate in local currency and may be subject to change without prior notification.
- Applicant shall be charged a service charge of AED 38.30 per application in addition to the visa fees and must be paid in cash or card (all applicable taxes included).
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Short stay visas (Schengen)
Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5YZwxLfUmPLr3oX2v8IGh8/c39f3701ced2d8c0cd96199ad9d123a7/student-visa-checklist-new.pdf) for the Student Visa checklist 

## Photo Specifications

Applicants need to submit your Application form with the new photo format, as explained below:  General  The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph provided does not meet these requirements, your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
The Student Visa application must be submitted 90 days before the intended travel date. Failing to do so will result in the application to be returned. The processing time can be at least 15 calendar days up to 90 days from the next working day of application submission.
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen Student visa at the Consulate General of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant. Third Countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation follow the below link:https://www.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/ingressosoggiornoinitalia/visto_ingresso/paesi_soggetti_visto/ (https://www.esteri.it/en/servizi-consolari-e-visti/ingressosoggiornoinitalia/visto_ingresso/paesi_soggetti_visto/)
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
The applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category. Kindly refer to the documents required page to make sure that you have all the supporting documents.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission. 
When booking an appointment for an Italy visa in Dubai, please note the following:
- Each user ID, including the associated email and mobile number, can be used to book only one appointment.
- Families with minors under the age of 12 who do not have an individual email address may bring the minor along with the parents’ confirmed appointment.
- A birth certificate or any document proving the relationship between the minor and the parents will be verified at the Visa Application Centre before entry is permitted.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Please click on Do I need a visa?  (http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en)to find out whether a Schengen visa is required or check on the
                                                                        interactive  map (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/index_en) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Consulate General of Italy, Dubai. Once the assessment is completed by the Consulate General of Italy the
                                                                        decision together with the passport will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Consulate General of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant. Third Countries whose nationals or specific categories of such third country nationals who are subject to prior consultation follow the below link (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/L9kMrPn0xyxFC7Hh5nU8W/6ee73a15f2c8c1b3596e80f88e98c48a/annex-16-prior-consultation-en-2022.pdf)
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience.
Schengen visa must be obtained from the Embassy/Consulate of the country where the main purpose of travel lies. Therefore, please check your travel plans before submitting your application. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please make your application to the one where you will be staying for the maximum number of days. If the duration of stay is the same in more than one Schengen country during the first trip, you must submit your visa application at the Embassy/Consulate of your first point of entry.
Please book appointments well in advance depending on your intended date of travel to avoid any inconvenience. Applications cannot be submitted earlier than six (6) months before the travel date. Please refer to the processing time specific to this Mission. 
When booking an appointment for an Italy visa in Dubai, please note the following:
- Each user ID, including the associated email and mobile number, can be used to book only one appointment.
- Families with minors under the age of 12 who do not have an individual email address may bring the minor along with the parents’ confirmed appointment.
- A birth certificate or any document proving the relationship between the minor and the parents will be verified at the Visa Application Centre before entry is permitted.

                                                                        interactive  map (https://home-affairs.ec.europa.eu/index_en) to learn more about
                                                                        the Schengen area. 
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Consulate General of Italy, Dubai. Once the assessment is completed by the Consulate General of Italy the
                                                                        decision together with the passport will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Note: The processing time for assessment of a Schengen visa at the Consulate General of Italy varies according to the nationality of the applicant.
